analysis_result: NF1 (MAF-only, non-synonymous) = 22/54 (40.7%). Paper reports 55% in Fig 2b oncoprint which includes 17q/NF1 locus deletions as 'Deletion' events alongside point mutations.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM5 (Supplementary Table 2): MAF-format WES variant table, 7457 rows, 123 columns, 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes.
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 rows; 54 samples marked WES='X'.
   - Paper Fig 2b oncoprint: n = 54 WES samples with 10 genes listed including NF1 at 55%.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - WES cohort: 54 samples (from MOESM4, WES='X').
   - Note: MOESM5 MAF contains only 52 unique samples (two WES samples absent from MAF, consistent with samples having no called variants passing filters).

C. VARIABLE CODING / VARIANT CALLS
   - Model definition: unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes in MAF with Hugo_Symbol='NF1' restricted to non-synonymous Variant_Classification values (Missense/Nonsense/Frame_Shift_Del/Frame_Shift_Ins/In_Frame_Del/In_Frame_Ins/Splice_Site/Nonstop/Translation_Start_Site).
   - Paper's Fig 2b legend defines alteration types: Deletion (red), Indel (black), Gain (green), Truncating (red), Missense (orange). 'Deletion' is a CNV event, not a MAF indel.

D. COMPUTATION
   - MAF-only NF1: 24 unique samples with any NF1 variant; 24 non-synonymous; all FILTER=PASS and TGL_FILTER_VERDICT=PASS.
   - MAF-only fraction: 24/54 = 44.4% (model-stated 22/54 = 40.7% is close; minor discrepancy with our exact recount of 24/54 likely reflects different non-synonymous inclusion rules).
   - Paper's reported 55% of 54 = ~30 samples with NF1 alteration.
   - Gap: 30 - 24 = 6 additional samples with NF1 loss (CNV) but no coding NF1 mutation.

E. RESULT
   - Reported: 55% (Fig 2b) = ~30/54.
   - Model: 40.7% (MAF-only).
   - Relative difference: |55 - 40.7| / 55 = 26.0% -> DISCREPANT (>10% relative), MAJOR (>20%).

F. ROOT CAUSE
   The discrepancy reflects a VARIABLE_CHOICE / data-integration issue: the paper's Fig 2b oncoprint explicitly combines point mutations (Indel, Truncating, Missense) with CNV losses ('Deletion') and gains. The paper text (p.4) states 'we observed significantly higher rates of 17q deletions encompassing the NF1 locus in MPNST-G1 compared to MPNST-G2,' confirming that NF1 locus deletions (called from CONUMEE on methylation data) contribute to the 55% figure. The model used MOESM5 MAF alone, which captures only coding SNVs/indels and excludes CNV deletion calls.

G. PROPOSED FIX
   Integrate CNV loss calls at the NF1 locus (chr17q11.2) from the methylation-derived CONUMEE CNV analysis (or MOESM8 fusion data if NF1-disrupting fusions are also tabulated) with the MAF non-synonymous calls. Union of (coding NF1 mutation) OR (17q/NF1 locus deletion) across the 54 WES samples should yield ~30/54 = 55%.
proposed_fix: Combine MAF non-synonymous NF1 mutations with CNV-based NF1/17q deletion calls (from CONUMEE methylation CNV analysis) to reproduce the paper's oncoprint alteration frequency. The Fig 2b legend explicitly includes 'Deletion' as an alteration type alongside Indel/Truncating/Missense, and the paper text confirms 17q deletions encompassing the NF1 locus contribute to the 55% figure.
